name: La Selva de Irués Trail
name_original: La Selva de Irués – circular desde Escalona (ZZ-008)
description: This moderate 17.9-mile mountain biking loop from Escalona offers 1964 feet of elevation gain and takes around 2 hours 47 minutes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Puértolas offers diverse terrain and elevation within the Aragonese Pyrenees. The region is characterized by rugged landscapes, dramatic canyons like Añisclo Canyon, and the majestic peaks of the Monte Perdido Massif. Cyclists encounter a variety of paths, from challenging unpaved segments with significant elevation changes to more accessible trails, providing a comprehensive mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails around Puértolas The most popular mountain bike trail is La Selva de Irués Trail, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through varied forest and open terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Pueyo de Araguás – Las Margas Trail loop from Laspuña, a difficult 27.2 miles (43.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Rocky trail towards Ainsa – El Pueyo de Araguás loop from Laspuña, a 23.7 miles (38.1 km) trail leading through rugged mountain terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Puértolas is defined by dramatic canyons, high mountain trails, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to varied descents. The routes in Puértolas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 7000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Puértolas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Puértolas?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Puértolas. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate forest paths to challenging high-mountain loops.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Puértolas?
A: Mountain biking in Puértolas caters to experienced riders, with 9 out of 13 routes rated as difficult. There are also 4 moderate trails for those seeking a less intense but still engaging ride. The region's rugged terrain and significant elevation changes contribute to the challenging nature of many routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Puértolas?
A: While many routes in Puértolas are challenging, the region does offer diverse terrain. For families, it's best to look for trails with less elevation gain and smoother surfaces. The Añisclo Canyon, for example, has some paved sections that might be suitable for less experienced riders, though specific family-friendly MTB routes are not explicitly listed in the data. Always check the route details for elevation and surface type before heading out with family.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Puértolas?
A: Puértolas is nestled in the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering dramatic landscapes. You can expect to encounter impressive gorges like the Añisclo Canyon, with its vertical walls and the Bellós River, and majestic peaks of the Monte Perdido Massif. Many routes provide panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape. You might also discover natural monuments, waterfalls, and lakes, enhancing the scenic beauty of your ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes around Puértolas are designed as loops. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24317581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pueyo de Araguás – Las Margas Trail loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocky trail towards Ainsa – El Pueyo de Araguás loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options. Another moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Cubierta Monument – Aínsa Old Town loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Puértolas?
A: The mountain bike trails around Puértolas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Over 7,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, rewarding descents, and the stunning Pyrenean vistas.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: While riding, you might encounter various points of interest. Some routes pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Cubierta Monument&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/322882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soaso Steps Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque de Gavarnie&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in mountain refuges, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Góriz Refuge&lt;/a&gt; is also in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Monte Perdido Massif?
A: Yes, Puértolas is situated near the Parque Nacional Ordesa y Monte Perdido, and many high mountain trails in the region offer spectacular views of the Monte Perdido Massif. Routes with significant elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24317581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pueyo de Araguás – Las Margas Trail loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt;, are likely to provide panoramic vistas of these majestic peaks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Puértolas?
A: The Aragonese Pyrenees, where Puértolas is located, experience distinct seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions, making them ideal for mountain biking. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, but higher mountain trails might be more accessible. Winter brings snow, limiting access to many routes, particularly those in the high mountains.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, Puértolas offers several longer routes for endurance riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Cubierta Monument – Aínsa Old Town loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 59 kilometers, providing a substantial ride through varied terrain. Other difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Trough in La Lera Forest – Peña Solano Viewpoint loop from Laspuña&lt;/a&gt; also offer significant distances and elevation.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region around Puértolas is rich in natural beauty, including waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/322882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soaso Steps Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, located within the nearby Gorges, are a notable natural attraction that can be explored in conjunction with mountain biking, though they may require a short detour or hike from specific bike routes.
tips: This track is part of the trans-Pyrenean mountain bike route. It marches between the Cotiella and the Peña Montañesa through a leafy valley with little traffic.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Selva de Irués – circular desde Escalona (ZZ-008) mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 28.8 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The La Selva de Irués – circular desde Escalona (ZZ-008) route is rated as moderate. While it features a significant elevation gain of nearly 600 meters, it is generally manageable for mountain bikers with some experience. Riders should be prepared for varied terrain and sustained climbs.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting and ending in Escalona. You can typically find parking within the village of Escalona, which serves as the primary access point for the trailhead. Specific parking areas might be signposted for trail users.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the La Selva de Irués mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain on this route, typical of mountain biking in the Pyrenees. It will likely include a mix of paths, tracks, and some road sections. Given the 'Selva' (forest) in its name, you'll likely encounter sections through beech and oak forests, which can offer both smooth and potentially technical riding. It's generally not recommended to ride in muddy conditions, and snow would make it impassable.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to mountain bike La Selva de Irués – circular desde Escalona (ZZ-008)?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are generally during the spring, summer, and autumn months. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be good, but be mindful of higher temperatures. Winter riding is not recommended due to potential snow and muddy conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the La Selva de Irués mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on mountain biking trails in this region, but they should always be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to ensure their safety, the safety of other trail users, and to protect local wildlife. Always clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Zona Zero Pirineos network. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for individual trails like this one, it's always a good idea to check the official Zona Zero Pirineos website for any specific regulations, permits, or recommendations regarding trail usage, especially for larger groups or events.

faqs.question: What points of interest or scenic views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter natural beauty and potentially historical sites. Notable highlights include the Fuente Selva Track, which suggests scenic forest sections, and the historic Santa María Church. The descent through beech and oak forests is often a highlight, offering picturesque views of the mountainous landscape.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other mountain biking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails in the region. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT (El Pont de Suert - Broto), Tella - Lafortunada - Ceresa - Laspuña - Ermita La Fuensanta, Senderos del Sobrarbe, and Sobrarbe. BTT. Ruta 10. De Laspuña a Araguás, among others. This indicates a well-connected trail network.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike trail of this length and elevation, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes, pump, and basic tools), a first-aid kit, appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions, and a helmet. A GPS device or a downloaded map of the route is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restaurants or accommodation near the trailhead in Escalona?
faqs.answer: Yes, Escalona is a village that serves as a base for outdoor activities in the region. You can expect to find local restaurants, cafes, and various accommodation options, including guesthouses or small hotels, to support your visit before or after your ride.
